title: Author PROP-041-amendment-002-categories-FGH: add Category F (editorial section-split), Category G (forward-reference preview), and Category H (link-out for depth) to the sloppytoppy-rewrite taxonomy. Operator confirmed both F + G after first-run PUNT analysis surfaced the category gaps; H captures the operator's discipline rule that long WIN debunks should link to underlying research rather than expand inline.
scope_authority: Design amendment only (PROP-041-amendment-002). Operator reviews amendment, then operator + cowork-claude apply. Tinker authors the amendment, does NOT self-apply.
context: PROP-041 Phase 2 first run completed 2026-05-16 ~11:45Z. Run produced 1 RW + 4 PUNTs. The 4 PUNTs surfaced category-taxonomy gaps that the rewriter explicitly called out in punt_reason fields and in its run summary:

PUNT-001 (part4.html#p4-41-0, 4178w section block): 'Categories A/B/C/D/E individually do NOT reduce length when applied to a single <details> block — they restructure prose, not eliminate content. The underlying remediation for 4178-word sections is editorial — split the single <details id="p4-41-0"> into 4-6 sub-sections at natural argument boundaries.'

PUNT-002 (part6.html#p6-610-9, 2270w with 10 forward references): 'The dominant defect is forward_references, which Categories A/B/C/D/E do NOT address — forward-reference fixes require either (a) inlining the referenced content (which violates RWR-5 "no new claims" since the referenced content currently lives in another section) or (b) replacing the bare reference with a one-sentence preview that explains why the reader should follow it (a Category not in the spec).'

D-A: Approve Category F (editorial section-split). Rationale: PUNT-001's described mechanism is correct — Phase 2 cannot fix length-dominated section blocks via A-E restructure. Editorial split is the right move.

D-B: Approve Category G (forward-reference preview). Rationale: PUNT-002's described mechanism (replace bare 'See Part 5' with a 1-sentence preview that explains why the reader should follow it) is a real, low-risk rewrite move.

D-C: Content-dense flag policy clarified — WIN surfaces are NEVER content-dense eligible. Long WIN debunks should link to underlying research rather than expand inline. (Applied in commit 4cbcecb; trimmed content-dense-surfaces.json 9 → 1.)
D-D: Onboarding batch_cap 5 → 3 during curmudgeon-calibration period. (Applied in commit 4cbcecb.)

The new Category H emerges from D-C: when a WIN surface is over target_max, the rewriter's lightest-touch fix should consider replacing depth content with a 1-sentence summary plus a link to the underlying research or to a longer-form analysis page. This is a behavior tinker should spec into rewriter discipline.
operator_intent: Tinker authors PROP-041-amendment-002-categories-FGH that adds three new categories (F editorial-split, G forward-ref preview, H link-out for depth) with full specs for each: what defects each category addresses, what the rewriter actually does mechanically, what content_preservation_audit fields are affected, how curmudgeon validates (new RWR entries), and any new data plumbing needed (e.g., F may need a new schema field for proposed sub-section boundaries). The amendment is reviewed by operator before any of those edits land.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Spec Category F (editorial section-split) in full. Key design questions to resolve:
questions: F-Q1: Does Category F propose new <details> blocks (modifying sections.json schema and HTML generation), or does it only flag the surface for operator/analyst attention with a proposed split-plan?
F-Q2: If F proposes new blocks, what's the RW output shape? A single RW-NNN.json with multiple rewritten_text fields per proposed sub-block? Or one parent RW + multiple child RW-NNN.A, RW-NNN.B etc?

F-Q3: How does curmudgeon validate an F rewrite? The RWR checklist has to verify that the split boundaries are at natural argument breaks, that no content is lost or reordered, and that each sub-block independently scores well.

F-Q4: F changes the surface set itself (one surface becomes 4-6). How does this interact with content_hash, scores.json record-keeping, and the cooldown_days mechanism? Does the parent surface get a 'replaced_by_split' status, or stays scored against the merged content?

F-Q5: Is F WIN-eligible (could a 2000-word WIN.detail_evidence be split into multiple WIN surfaces?), or section-block only? Operator's policy D-C says WINs should NOT expand inline — F-on-WIN would create new fields in wins.json, which is a bigger architectural change. Tinker recommendation expected.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Full F spec including the answers to F-Q1 through F-Q5 with rationale.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Spec Category G (forward-reference preview). Should be lower-stakes than F. Key design questions:
questions: G-Q1: What's the rewriter's mechanical move? Take 'See Section 1.8' and replace with 'Section 1.8 closes the refraction-fix escape — H(r) cannot be reinterpreted as apparent without invalidating the Polaris calibration.' What's the prompt-level instruction for finding the preview content? Read the referenced section? Use the referenced section's TLDR? Use the referenced section's <summary>?
G-Q2: How does this interact with RWR-5 ('no new claims')? Is the preview considered a paraphrase of existing content (no new claim) or a new framing (new claim)? Recommend tinker draws a clear line.
G-Q3: Does Category G require reading multiple files (wins.json + sections.json to find the referenced content)? If so, that's a token-cost increase — note in cost analysis.
G-Q4: What's the failure mode when a forward reference points to content that doesn't exist or has been deleted (drift)? Punt? Replace with bracketed-question?
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Full G spec including answers to G-Q1 through G-Q4.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Spec Category H (link-out for depth). This is the operator's discipline rule made into a rewriter category. Key design questions:
questions: H-Q1: What's the trigger? Only fires when the surface is over X× target_max (suggest 1.5× as threshold)? Or any surface where the rewriter judges that 'depth content' can be replaced with a summary + link?

H-Q2: What's the link destination? Existing dome-review pages (cross-link to our own deep-dive content)? External research citations (already in citations_preserved — would they get promoted from inline-cite to as-link)? A new dedicated page (out of scope for Category H, becomes Category F)?

H-Q3: What's the content-preservation discipline? If H replaces a 400-word depth section with 'Detail in [linked-page]', then numbers_preserved and citations_preserved both shrink — they no longer appear in rewritten_text, but they do appear in the linked content. Does the audit script need to follow links? Probably no — H's audit becomes verdict_unchanged + claim_unchanged + argument_structure_summary, with numbers/citations explicitly NOT preserved (the content was extracted to the linked page).

H-Q4: How does this interact with D-C operator policy? D-C says WINs shouldn't expand inline. H is the operational mechanism for D-C — when a long WIN is rewrite-eligible, the rewriter SHOULD reach for H first.
H-Q5: Is there an inverse risk? Linking out too aggressively could fragment the argument across pages, making the dome review harder to read, not easier. Counter-argument expected.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Full H spec including answers to H-Q1 through H-Q5, with a clear counter-argument section addressing fragmentation risk.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Identify and spec the edits to existing files needed to add F/G/H:
files_likely_affected: monitor/prompts/sloppytoppy-rewrite.md — add F/G/H to the category enum and the rewriter's per-category mechanical instructions
monitor/prompts/reference/sloppytoppy-rewrite-rubric.md — add RWR-10/11/12 (or extend existing RWRs) covering F/G/H verification
monitor/prompts/reference/DATA-SCHEMAS.md — RW-NNN.json schema may need new fields (e.g., F.proposed_block_boundaries, G.preview_source_ref, H.linked_destination_url)
monitor/scripts/audit-rewrite.js — may need new mechanical checks (e.g., H's modified content-preservation rules)
CLAUDE.md — if Category F creates new sections.json fields, update the data-flow section
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: For each affected file, identify the patch shape (old_string/new_string or full new content). Use the same shape as PROP-041-amendment-001-implementation patches.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Verify no regression against PROP-041 amendment-001 (just landed): no NEVER_PUSH writers introduced, no append-only stable filename collisions, audit-script changes are backward-compatible with existing RW-001, F's potential sections.json edits don't conflict with existing analyst-side conventions for wins.json/sections.json writes.
Per operator preference, include a counter-argument section steelmanning the case for NOT adding all three categories. Strongest counters:
counters_to_address: 'F is an editorial decision, not a mechanical rewrite. Pushing it into the rewriter agent inverts the operator-as-content-architect model. F should be human-decision, not Opus-decision.' Rebuttal expected: F is propose-only — the rewriter SUGGESTS split boundaries; operator + curmudgeon approve. Same review model as A-E.

'G requires the rewriter to read the referenced section, which is a token-cost increase that could double the per-surface cost. Worth it?' Rebuttal expected: forward_references is the second-largest understandability subtraction across all below-floor surfaces (42 of 168, 25%). Fixing it pays dividends across the queue.

'H risks fragmenting the review — readers get a teaser + a link, click through, lose context. The current inline-everything design is a deliberate choice for readability.' Rebuttal expected: D-C operator policy supersedes — for WIN surfaces specifically, the inline-everything design is over-engineered. Sectioning that long-detail material to a separate page is correct.

in_scope_for_tinker: Read PROP-041 + amendment-001-implementation + the first-run PUNT records (PUNT-001 through PUNT-004) to ground the F/G/H specs in concrete observed patterns
Read DATA-SCHEMAS.md current RW schema to identify needed extensions for F/G/H
Author one amendment (PROP-041-amendment-002-categories-FGH) with full specs for all three categories
Include regression check section + counter-argument section
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify any prompt or script directly. Amendment-only.

Do NOT propose a 4th or 5th category in this amendment — F/G/H is the operator-approved set. If the regression check or counter-arg analysis surfaces a need for additional categories, file as a separate amendment-003.
Do NOT register a new scheduled task. F/G/H are additions to the existing dome-sloppytoppy-rewrite agent's category enum, not new agents.
operator_preferences_for_amendment: Single comprehensive amendment for all three categories
Phased patch groupings (which patches are safe to apply standalone vs which depend on others)
Explicit policy-D-C alignment for Category H (the link-out-for-depth category is the operational mechanism for the WIN-content-dense-exclusion policy)

F is the most architecturally risky — spec it carefully, including how multi-block splits interact with sections.json + scores.json + cooldown + content_hash. If F turns out to be too architecturally invasive for Phase 2, propose splitting F into Phase 2.1 in the amendment, leaving G+H in Phase 2.

addendum_20260516_compression_experiment.source: EXP-401 compression experiment + operator confirmation 'much better'
addendum_20260516_compression_experiment.finding: Content compression IS qualitatively different from mechanical rewriting (RW-001 style). The WIN-012 compression (1888w → 528w, 3.58×) preserved all load-bearing numbers, citations, and verdict while producing tighter prose. Operator confirmed the compressed version reads better. Integrated into wins.json at commit 66c9a74.
design_implication_for_amendment_002: Category H ('link-out for depth') was originally framed as a rewriter-side category — the rewriter replaces a depth paragraph with a 1-sentence summary + link. The compression experiment shows this framing is too narrow: REAL content compression requires editorial judgment about which arguments are load-bearing, where cut content belongs (other WINs, section blocks, follow-up ISSs), and whether some cuts deserve to be flagged as 'structurally separate' (the EXP-401 GRACE Exhibit-A cut became ISS-2027 because it was a separate argument that didn't belong in WIN-012 at all).

Tinker's amendment-002 should reflect this: Category H stays in the rewriter category set, but its scope is NARROW — only mechanical link-out moves (e.g., 'Section 1.8 closes the refraction-fix escape — H(r) cannot be reinterpreted as apparent...' replacing bare 'See Section 1.8'). For real content compression, the right home is ANALYST as an HNOTE-commissioned mode (or eventually a new analyst Mode 6 if compression becomes recurring work).

The rewriter should still PUNT length-dominated WIN surfaces when A-E (or H-as-narrow-mechanical) cannot bring the surface above acceptable_floor — the PUNT record then surfaces the need for analyst-commissioned compression to the operator. This is the right division of labor: rewriter handles mechanical/structural defects; analyst handles editorial-judgment compression.

Counter-argument tinker should address in amendment-002: 'If content compression is HNOTE-commissioned analyst work, the analyst dispatcher needs to actually consume HNOTEs at a reasonable cadence. The current Mode 2 placement (behind Mode 1's tracker queue) means operator HNOTEs sit for hours-to-days behind routine work. The compression mode is only practical if HNOTE-pickup is fast. Separately, this is DIRECTIVE-20260516-006 work — tinker should flag the dependency but not solve dispatcher ordering inside amendment-002.'
what_this_changes_in_amendment_002_specs: Q-OP F-Q5 (Is F WIN-eligible?): operator's content-dense policy (D-C, 2026-05-16) plus the compression experiment converge on 'F should NOT be WIN-eligible'. F (editorial section-split) applies to section_details_block surfaces only. WIN surfaces that need 3-5x compression go through analyst-compression-mode commission, NOT F.

Q-OP H-Q1 (threshold?): narrow scope. H fires only when there's a SPECIFIC bare forward-reference or a clearly-cited 'see <other-page>' link the rewriter is replacing with a preview. H does NOT replace whole paragraphs with link-outs — that's compression-territory.
Q-OP H-Q4 (D-C alignment?): H is NOT the operational mechanism for D-C. D-C's operational mechanism is analyst-compression-mode (HNOTE-commissioned). H stays as a mechanical link-preview move.
Q-OP H-Q5 (fragmentation risk?): under the narrow H scope above, fragmentation risk is minimal — H only adds preview sentences, doesn't relocate content to other pages.

experiment_artifacts.follow_up_iss: ISS-2027 (GRACE Exhibit-A graph forensics → own surface)
